subject: Chemistry
title: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

questions.text: The Modern Periodic Law states that the properties of elements are a periodic function of their:
questions.explanation: Modern periodic law was proposed by Moseley based on atomic number (Z).

questions.text: Which of the following elements has the highest electronegativity?
questions.explanation: Fluorine is the most electronegative element on the Pauling scale (4.0).

questions.text: Elements in the same group of the periodic table have the same:
questions.explanation: Chemical properties are similar because they share the same valence shell configuration.

questions.text: The general electronic configuration of d-block elements is:
questions.explanation: Electrons fill the penultimate d-subshell and the outermost s-subshell.

questions.text: Across a period from left to right, the atomic radius generally:
questions.explanation: Increased effective nuclear charge pulls electrons closer to the nucleus.

questions.text: Which group contains the noble gases?
questions.explanation: Group 18 elements have completely filled valence shells and are chemically inert.

questions.text: The element with atomic number 117 would belong to which family?
questions.explanation: Z=117 is Tennessine, located in Group 17 (Halogens).

questions.text: Ionization enthalpy generally _____ down a group.
questions.explanation: As the size increases, the outer electron is further from the nucleus and easier to remove.

questions.text: Which of the following is an amphoteric oxide?
questions.explanation: Aluminum oxide reacts with both acids and bases.

questions.text: The IUPAC name for element with Z=104 is:
questions.explanation: Un(1) + nil(0) + quad(4) + ium.

questions.text: Representative elements belong to:
questions.explanation: Main group elements (excluding noble gases).

questions.text: Diagonal relationship is shown between:
questions.explanation: Due to similar ionic sizes and charge/radius ratios.

questions.text: The most metallic elements are found in:
questions.explanation: Metallic character increases down a group and decreases across a period.

questions.text: Which has the smallest size?
questions.explanation: Isoelectronic species: higher nuclear charge results in smaller size.

questions.text: Screening effect is also known as:
questions.explanation: Inner electrons shield outer electrons from the nucleus.

questions.text: Lanthanoids and Actinoids are placed in:
questions.explanation: Inner transition elements.

questions.text: Which element has the highest electron gain enthalpy (most negative)?
questions.explanation: Chlorine has less inter-electronic repulsion than Fluorine.

questions.text: Transuranium elements start after:
questions.explanation: Elements with Z > 92 are synthetic and follow Uranium.

questions.text: Which period is the shortest?
questions.explanation: Contains only Hydrogen and Helium.

questions.text: Effective nuclear charge (Zeff) is:
questions.explanation: Actual charge felt by outer electrons after shielding.

questions.text: The first ionization enthalpy of Nitrogen is higher than Oxygen due to:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen has 2p¬≥, a stable half-filled configuration.

questions.text: Metals form _____ oxides.
questions.explanation: Example: Na‚āāO + H‚āāO -> 2NaOH.

questions.text: Non-metals form _____ oxides.
questions.explanation: Example: SO‚āÉ + H‚āāO -> H‚āāSO‚āĄ.

questions.text: Valency of group 1 elements is:
questions.explanation: They lose one electron to reach noble gas configuration.

questions.text: As we move down a group, the non-metallic character:
questions.explanation: Easier to lose electrons means more metallic.

questions.text: The element with Z=12 is in which block?
questions.explanation: Magnesium: [Ne] 3s¬≤.

questions.text: How many groups are there in the long form of Periodic Table?
questions.explanation: Vertical columns 1 to 18.

questions.text: How many periods are there in the long form of Periodic Table?
questions.explanation: Horizontal rows 1 to 7.

questions.text: The most reactive metal is:
questions.explanation: Cesium has the lowest ionization enthalpy in the s-block.

questions.text: The most reactive non-metal is:
questions.explanation: Due to high electronegativity and high electron gain enthalpy.

questions.text: Which block elements are all metals?
questions.explanation: Only p-block contains non-metals and metalloids.

questions.text: Metalloids are also called:
questions.explanation: Elements showing properties of both metals and non-metals.

questions.text: Ionic radius of a cation is _____ than its parent atom.
questions.explanation: Loss of electrons increases effective nuclear charge per electron.

questions.text: Ionic radius of an anion is _____ than its parent atom.
questions.explanation: Extra electron increases repulsion and reduces Zeff.

questions.text: The group 17 elements are known as:
questions.explanation: Salt-formers.

questions.text: The d-block elements are known as:
questions.explanation: They 'transition' between s and p blocks.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a transition element?
questions.explanation: Zinc has fully filled d-orbitals in both atomic and common ionic states.

questions.text: Group 16 elements are called:
questions.explanation: Ore-forming elements.

questions.text: Unit of electronegativity is:
questions.explanation: It is a relative scale/tendency, not an absolute energy value.

questions.text: Who is the father of the periodic table?
questions.explanation: Dmitri Mendeleev published the first widely recognized periodic table.
